algorithms.title: BLS Assessment & Recognition of Cardiac Arrest
algorithms.description: The stepwise sequence a BLS provider uses from arrival to the decision to start CPR.
steps: Ensure the scene is safe before approaching; address or avoid hazards.
Check responsiveness: tap the shoulders (sole of the foot for an infant) and shout.
If unresponsive, shout for nearby help and activate the emergency response system.
Send someone to retrieve an AED and additional equipment (or use a phone on speaker if alone).
Simultaneously check for normal breathing and a pulse for no more than 10 seconds (carotid in adult/child, brachial in infant).
Interpret findings: normal breathing + pulse → monitor and reassess; pulse but inadequate breathing → give breaths; no pulse / no normal breathing / unsure → begin CPR.
If in arrest, start high-quality chest compressions immediately and apply the AED as soon as it arrives.
